ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general waffle food product entry; no specific ice cream waffle composite entry exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKW covers waffles as a category; no composite ice cream waffle code exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for ice cream waffles composite food. Left null to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving consists of one waffle (approximately 100g) topped or served alongside one scoop of ice cream (approximately 50g), totalling around 150g.

nutrient_source.title: Ice Cream Waffles — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Ice Cream Waffles (cr_id F11100010) with confidence 0.95. All core macros and secondary nutrients are from this source.

gi_source.title: Waffles, plain — GI value 76 (analogous); Waffle with ice cream composite estimated GI 57 based on ice cream moderating effect on waffle GI per Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for ice cream waffles as a composite dish. GI of 57 is derived from the HPB Foodo embedded GI database value (confidence 0.794), which is consistent with the moderating effect of ice cream fat and protein on the glycaemic response of the waffle base. Plain waffles have a GI of approximately 76 (University of Sydney GI Database). Ice cream (full-fat) has a GI of approximately 37–51. The composite GI of 57 is a defensible analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI × carbohydrate per serving (g) / 100 = 57 × 59.58g / 100 = 33.9 (based on 150g serving containing 39.72g carbohydrate per 100g).

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (19.37g/100g) with low dietary fibre (1.1g/100g) drives insulin-mediated sodium retention; saturated fat (~8g/100g) contributes to arterial stiffness
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit ice cream waffles due to the high sugar and saturated fat content, which promote insulin-mediated sodium retention and vascular stiffness; avoid additional syrups or toppings.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High total carbohydrate (39.72g/100g) and sugar (19.37g/100g) with minimal dietary fibre (1.1g/100g) cause rapid postprandial glucose elevation; medium GI of 57 with high glycemic load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid ice cream waffles as the high sugar and refined carbohydrate content with minimal fibre buffering causes rapid postprandial glucose spikes; if consumed, portion size must be strictly limited and paired with high-fibre foods.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~8g/100g) and trans fat (0.1g/100g) elevate LDL cholesterol; high sugar (19.37g/100g) promotes triglyceride synthesis and endothelial inflammation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid ice cream waffles due to the combination of saturated fat, trans fat, and high sugar, which collectively raise LDL cholesterol, promote triglyceride synthesis, and contribute to endothelial dysfunction.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat (~8g/100g) exceeds the 5g/100g RA threshold; refined sugar (19.37g/100g) promotes pro-inflammatory cytokines; trace trans fat (0.1g/100g) is strongly pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid ice cream waffles as the high saturated fat, refined sugar, and trace trans fat content collectively promote systemic inflammation and may exacerbate joint symptoms.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ice cream waffles is a popular Singaporean snack and dessert consisting of a waffle made from pancake-style batter — typically containing flour, eggs, milk, butter, and sugar — cooked in a waffle iron until golden and crisp on the outside, then served with one or more scoops of ice cream. The waffle provides a warm, slightly crispy and chewy texture that contrasts with the cold, creamy ice cream. Common ice cream flavours include vanilla, chocolate, and local favourites such as yam (taro) and red bean. The dish is widely sold at hawker centres, food courts, and dessert stalls across Singapore. Nutritionally, ice cream waffles are energy-dense at 315 kcal per 100g, with a high carbohydrate content of 39.72g/100g, of which 19.37g is sugar, and a fat content of 15.24g/100g including approximately 8g saturated fat and trace trans fat. Dietary fibre is low at 1.1g/100g. The glycemic index is estimated at 57 (medium), but the glycemic load per 150g serving is high at approximately 33.9, making this food unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. Sodium is moderate at 110mg/100g. Calcium is present at 65mg/100g from the dairy components. This food is not suitable for vegans and contains gluten, milk, and egg allergens.
common_user_queries: How many calories in ice cream waffles?
Are ice cream waffles suitable for diabetics?
Is waffle with ice cream healthy?
Can I eat ice cream waffles if I have high blood pressure?
What is the GI of ice cream waffles?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database with confidence 0.95 for all macros and secondary nutrients. The food is a canonical HPB entry (cr_id F11100010) with reviewed provenance. GI is an analogous estimate from the HPB embedded GI database (confidence 0.794), consistent with University of Sydney GI Database values for component foods.
notes: Fields set to -1 indicate data not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo source for this entry: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g — detailed micronutrient and fatty acid fractionation data for this composite dish are not reported in the HPB Foodo database as of 2026. Trans fat value of 0.1g/100g is an estimate consistent with the presence of butter and ice cream in the recipe; this value is not explicitly reported in the HPB source and is flagged as an approximation. Saturated fat of approximately 8g/100g is estimated from the known composition of butter and ice cream components; not explicitly reported in the HPB source. Halal status is not confirmed as ice cream and waffle ingredients may vary by vendor. GI of 57 is an analogous estimate from the HPB embedded GI database, consistent with the moderating effect of ice cream fat and protein on the waffle base GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated at 150g serving size: GL = 57 × (39.72 × 1.5) / 100 = 57 × 59.58 / 100 ≈ 33.9.
